pages.full_text:
early paintings were [?some] figurative, later ones abstract. We like his work, but friends, whose work he had collected, seemed rather derivative. Beautiful old house with lovely views of lake.

      We stopped on our way to the van at a splendid corner shop. The owner gave me a book on local sights & post cards.

      We came back to the camp for lunch. I like this camp very much. They seem to welcome children & people of all ages, & dogs. There are group of children of various ages messing about all the time. 2 brothers seem to own it. It was their father's house, he inherited it, but they were born in Peru. One brother does the reception & cleans Sinks & general upkeep, ordering the groundsmen. The other brother, with his Dutch wife, who has dark hair, does the bar. So they speaks Italian & English fluently & the woman speaks Dutch & English pretty well. We seem to be the only campers who are not Dutch or Italian. There's a jar of ping pong balls in
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from a previous page; text begins mid-narrative. The word before 'figurative' is partially obscured — reads like 'some' or similar struck-through word. No handwritten page number visible at top-left corner. Year cannot be determined from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
ones reception ready for the children who playground robin on the ping pong table in the evening. The pool is fenced off but always busy.

      In the afternoon we read & clean the van. I feel back to normal now after 10 days of a bad tummy. Still need to sit down sometimes.

      When we originally got to __Camp Casio__, I said to Simon that what I really wanted to see was the jazz concert on the Saturday, but I knew that we wouldn't be here that long... But we are! And we went to the concert & it was marvellous. A very good guitar player, 2 other guitar, a, sax & a violin. They played about 6.30 - 7.45 & 8.45 - 10. BUT there was a thunderstorm
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from left page; text begins mid-sentence ('ones reception ready...'). Two photographs glued in at the bottom of the page: one appears to show an ornate interior (possibly a palace or theatre with frescoes/murals), the other shows a medieval castle with towers. No captions visible for either photo. The campsite name 'Camp Casio' is underlined in the original — could also be read as 'Camp Casino' or 'Casio' — transcribed as written. Text ends mid-sentence 'BUT there was a thunderstorm'. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume covers 2003-2019.
